findings.title: ContextMerger and EntityExtractor have no live consumer
findings.description: ContextMerger and EntityExtractor are exported from the context-engine barrel (index.ts) but no module in apps/ or any other package imports them. The classes are dead-on-arrival: no HTTP handler, event listener, cron job, or Electron service instantiates them. Grep across the entire monorepo (apps/, packages/, services/) returns zero import matches outside context-engine itself and its tests.
findings.suggestion: Wire ContextMerger into an existing live entry point, such as the AnimaOrchestrator in apps/stage-tamagotchi/src/main/services/anima/orchestrator.ts, which already consumes other context-engine modules (ScreenshotPipeline, ActivityMonitor). Even a minimal call like `orchestrator.getMergedContext()` would prove the wiring works end-to-end.

findings.title: Test description says 'weighted average' but code uses simple mean
findings.description: The test name states 'weighted average' but the actual implementation in computeImportance() (context-merger.ts:72-81) computes a simple arithmetic mean: sum / count. A weighted average would weight each source differently (e.g., by recency or source type). The JSDoc on computeImportance correctly says 'average' without the 'weighted' qualifier. This discrepancy will mislead anyone reading the test into thinking the importance calculation accounts for per-source weights, when it does not.
findings.suggestion: Rename the test to: it('computes importance as arithmetic mean of source importances', ...) or simply it('computes importance as average of source importances', ...) to match the actual behavior and the JSDoc.

findings.title: Error messages lack operational context (source count, text length)
findings.description: Both ContextMerger.merge() (line 155) and EntityExtractor.extract() (line 33 of entity-extractor.ts) wrap errors with new Error(msg, { cause }), which is the correct pattern. However, neither message includes the operational context needed for debugging: number of sources, source types, combined text length, or a text snippet. When these errors surface in production logs, the developer cannot tell what input triggered the failure without digging through the cause chain -- and the cause is typically a raw LLM SDK error with no input context either.
findings.suggestion: Include input context in both error messages. For ContextMerger: `throw new Error(`Context merge failed (${selected.length} sources, types=[${selected.map(s=>s.source).join(',')}])`, { cause })`. For EntityExtractor: `throw new Error(`Entity extraction failed (text length=${text.length})`, { cause })`.

findings.title: EntityExtractor trusts LLM output without runtime validation
findings.description: Encapsulation: 8/10, Expression: 5/10, Usefulness: 7/10, Enforcement: 2/10 (Aggregate: 5.5). EntityExtractor calls generateStructured<ExtractedEntities> but performs no runtime validation on the returned object. The generic type parameter T is erased at runtime -- if the LLM returns malformed data (e.g., a string instead of an array for 'persons', or a missing field), the invalid shape silently propagates through the pipeline. SmartTip in this same codebase demonstrates the gold-standard pattern: validate LLM output with type guards before returning.
findings.suggestion: Add a validateExtractedEntities() type guard that checks all 5 fields are present and are string arrays, similar to SmartTip's isValidSmartTipResult(). Return a safe default (empty arrays) or throw a typed error on validation failure.

findings.title: Horizontal-only change: no vertical slice integration
findings.description: This task adds a complete context-merging layer (ContextMerger, EntityExtractor, types) with tests, but delivers zero vertical integration. There is no entry point that calls ContextMerger.merge(), no IPC handler exposes it to the UI, and no scheduled job triggers it. This is a horizontal layer addition (pure library code) rather than a vertical slice that proves end-to-end connectivity.
findings.suggestion: Add at least one vertical integration point: (1) call ContextMerger.merge() from AnimaOrchestrator when processing a context update cycle, passing the outputs of ScreenshotPipeline + ActivityMonitor as ContextSource[], or (2) add an IPC handler that the UI can invoke. Include one integration test proving the wiring.

findings.title: No validation of LLM structured output shape
findings.description: classifyActivity() (line 164-169) trusts that generateStructured returns an object with a string `activityType` field. If the LLM returns a malformed response (e.g., `{}`, `{ activityType: 123 }`, or `null`), `result.activityType` becomes `undefined` or a non-string value. This silently propagates through validateActivityType which only checks Set membership, causing it to return 'other' -- masking LLM misbehavior. Same issue in EntityExtractor: if the LLM omits a field, callers get `undefined` arrays that will throw on `.map()` or `.length` access downstream.
findings.suggestion: Add runtime validation after generateStructured calls. For classifyActivity: verify `typeof result?.activityType === 'string'` and throw a typed error if not. For EntityExtractor: verify all 5 arrays exist and are arrays. Consider using Valibot (project standard) for schema validation of LLM responses.

findings.title: combinedText built twice with identical logic
findings.description: The merge() method builds `combinedText` at line 130-131, then passes `selected` to generateSummary() which rebuilds the identical string at lines 173-175. The same string formatting logic `[${s.source}] ${s.summary}` is duplicated. This is a minor DRY violation that also means the LLM receives the same text computed twice.
findings.suggestion: Pass the already-computed `combinedText` string directly to generateSummary() instead of passing `sources` and recomputing. Change the signature to `private async generateSummary(combinedText: string): Promise<string>` and call `this.llm.generateText({ system: SUMMARY_SYSTEM_PROMPT, prompt: combinedText })`.

findings.title: No test for invalid activityType fallback to 'other'
findings.description: The test verifies all 7 valid activity types are passed through correctly, but there is no test for the fallback behavior when the LLM returns an invalid activity type string (e.g., 'gaming' or 'unknown'). The production code at context-merger.ts:183-188 has a validateActivityType method that defaults to 'other' for unrecognized types. This fallback logic is untested and could silently break if refactored.
findings.suggestion: Add a test case where the StubLlmProvider returns an invalid activity type (e.g., 'invalid_type') and assert that the merged result has activityType === 'other'.

findings.title: Unsafe `as ActivityType` cast in validateActivityType
findings.description: The `as ActivityType` cast at line 185 is technically safe because of the Set guard, but the VALID_ACTIVITY_TYPES Set is typed as `Set<string>` (line 16) rather than `Set<ActivityType>`. If someone adds a new ActivityType to the union but forgets to update the Set, the cast would silently allow the unvalidated value through. The Set and the type are not structurally linked.
findings.suggestion: Type the set as `Set<ActivityType>` to get compile-time enforcement: `const VALID_ACTIVITY_TYPES: ReadonlySet<ActivityType> = new Set<ActivityType>([...])`. This way, adding a new value to the ActivityType union without updating the Set would produce a type error.

findings.title: computeImportance clamp logic not tested with out-of-range values
findings.description: The test named 'clamps result to [0, 1]' only tests with valid boundary values (0 and 1). It does not test values outside the valid range (e.g., importance: 1.5 or importance: -0.3) that would actually exercise the Math.max(0, Math.min(1, ...)) clamping logic in computeImportance (context-merger.ts:80).
findings.suggestion: Add test cases with out-of-range importance values to verify the clamp actually works (e.g., importance: 1.5 should clamp to 1, importance: -0.5 should clamp to 0).

findings.title: MergedContext output type lacks readonly modifiers
findings.description: Encapsulation: 3/10, Expression: 5/10, Usefulness: 7/10, Enforcement: 3/10 (Aggregate: 4.5). MergedContext is a pipeline output type representing the merged result of multiple context sources. It should be immutable after construction. Task 19 established a new standard in context-engine: DocumentExtractionResult, TextChunk, and FileChangeEvent all use readonly fields. MergedContext breaks this pattern.
findings.suggestion: Add readonly to all fields and use readonly arrays for keywords, entities, and extractedEntities.

findings.title: No structured logging in ContextMerger or EntityExtractor
findings.description: Neither ContextMerger nor EntityExtractor include any structured logging. The merge pipeline makes 3 concurrent LLM calls (entity extraction, activity classification, summary generation), but there is no visibility into: how many sources were merged, how long each LLM call took, which step failed on error.
findings.suggestion: Add @guiiai/logg structured logging: (1) INFO at merge start with sourceCount, (2) DEBUG with LLM call durations, (3) ERROR in the catch block before re-throwing.

findings.title: validateActivityType silently falls back to 'other' without logging
findings.description: When the LLM returns an invalid activity type string (e.g., 'working', 'reading', a hallucinated value), validateActivityType silently maps it to 'other' with no log or warning. This masks LLM prompt drift or model degradation -- the system continues functioning but classification quality degrades invisibly.
findings.suggestion: Add a console.warn (or structured logger call) when falling back: `console.warn('Unknown activity type from LLM, defaulting to other', { received: type })`. This preserves the graceful fallback behavior while making the anomaly observable.

findings.title: ContextSource and ExtractedEntities lack readonly modifiers
findings.description: Encapsulation: 4/10, Expression: 6/10, Usefulness: 6/10, Enforcement: 3/10 (Aggregate: 4.75). Both ContextSource (input) and ExtractedEntities (output) are fully mutable. ContextSource is passed into merge() and should not be mutated by the merger. ExtractedEntities flows downstream as immutable data. The arrays (entities, keywords, persons, etc.) are particularly vulnerable to accidental mutation.
findings.suggestion: Mark all fields readonly. For ContextSource, at minimum make the array fields readonly. For ExtractedEntities, mark all 5 string array fields as readonly string[].

findings.title: StubLlmProvider routes by schema content string matching -- brittle
findings.description: The StubLlmProvider.generateStructured method routes its response by checking if schemaDescription.includes('persons'). This couples the test to an internal implementation detail (the exact schema description string). If the schema description changes, the stub will silently return the wrong response type.
findings.suggestion: Consider making the routing more explicit, for example by tracking call order (first call = entities, second = activity) or by using separate stub instances for each LLM call.

findings.title: Promise.all for independent LLM calls -- partial failure wastes work
findings.description: Three independent LLM calls run via Promise.all. If any one fails, the entire merge fails and the results from the other two (which may have succeeded) are discarded. A transient failure in one LLM call forces retry of all three calls.
findings.suggestion: If partial results are acceptable, use Promise.allSettled and populate fallback values for failed calls. If all-or-nothing is correct, document this choice with a comment explaining why partial merge is not useful.

findings.title: ContextSource.importance lacks type-level constraint for 0-1 range
findings.description: Encapsulation: 4/10, Expression: 4/10, Usefulness: 6/10, Enforcement: 3/10 (Aggregate: 4.25). The importance field is documented as '0-1' in JSDoc but typed as bare number. Callers can pass -5 or 100 without type error. computeImportance() clamps the output but does not validate inputs.
findings.suggestion: Add a validateContextSource() function at the boundary where ContextSource is created, asserting 0 <= importance <= 1.

findings.title: Unreachable catch guard for empty-sources error
findings.description: The empty-sources guard (`throw new Error('At least one context source is required')`) is on line 121, which is BEFORE the try block starts on line 124. Therefore this error can never be caught by the catch block. The string comparison on line 152 is dead code -- it will never match.
findings.suggestion: Remove the dead branch. Simplify the catch to just: `catch (cause) { throw new Error('Context merge failed', { cause }) }`.

findings.title: Duplicate source-to-text mapping on lines 131 and 174
findings.description: The pattern `.map(s => `[${s.source}] ${s.summary}`).join('\n')` appears identically at line 131 (in merge()) and line 174 (in generateSummary()). The merge method builds combinedText, then generateSummary rebuilds the exact same string from the same sources.
findings.suggestion: Pass the already-built combinedText string to generateSummary() instead of the sources array. Change signature to `private async generateSummary(combinedText: string): Promise<string>`.

findings.title: Redundant error identity check in catch block
findings.description: The catch block at line 151-156 checks if the caught error is the empty-sources validation error thrown at line 121 and re-throws it unwrapped. However, this validation error is thrown before any async work begins (line 120-122), so it exits the function before entering the try block. The string-matching guard is dead code.
findings.suggestion: Remove the identity check and simplify the catch to just: `catch (cause) { throw new Error('Context merge failed', { cause }) }`.

findings.title: Hardcoded count '7 activity types' in JSDoc will rot if types change
findings.description: The JSDoc comment on the ActivityType union hardcodes the number '7'. If an activity type is added or removed in the future, the comment becomes silently incorrect.
findings.suggestion: Remove the count: change to 'Activity types for user context classification.' The union definition itself makes the count self-evident.

findings.title: extractEntities is a single-line passthrough method
findings.description: The private method `extractEntities` is a trivial single-line delegation to `this.entityExtractor.extract(text)`. Unlike `classifyActivity` and `generateSummary` which contain LLM call construction logic, this method adds no transformation, no error handling, and no additional parameters.
findings.suggestion: Inline the call: replace `this.extractEntities(combinedText)` with `this.entityExtractor.extract(combinedText)` and remove the private method.

findings.title: ContextMerger does not validate maxSources option
findings.description: Encapsulation: 8/10, Expression: 7/10, Usefulness: 7/10, Enforcement: 6/10 (Aggregate: 7.0). The constructor accepts maxSources without validation. A caller could pass 0 or a negative number. With maxSources=0, selectTopSources returns [] but the empty-sources check has already passed.
findings.suggestion: Add a guard in the constructor: `if (max < 1) throw new RangeError('maxSources must be >= 1')`.

positive_observations: Clean Functional Core / Imperative Shell separation: pure utility functions (deduplicateStrings, mergeKeywords, computeImportance, selectTopSources) are properly extracted and independently testable without any mocking.

LLM calls use Test Doubles with proper rationale comments in both test files, consistent with project conventions for external API boundaries. No vi.fn(), vi.mock(), jest.fn(), jest.mock(), InMemoryRepository, MockXxx, FakeXxx, or skipped tests detected.
Comprehensive unit test coverage for all pure functions (context-merger-utils.test.ts) with edge cases: empty input, single element, whitespace trimming, case-insensitive dedup, clamping.
Error handling wraps LLM failures with contextual messages using the cause chain pattern (Error cause), enabling debuggable error traces.
Well-defined TypeScript interfaces (ContextSource, MergedContext, ExtractedEntities) with JSDoc documentation provide clear contracts for the merging pipeline.
Concurrent LLM calls via Promise.all for entity extraction, activity classification, and summary generation -- good performance optimization.
All exported pure functions have accurate JSDoc that explains behavior, edge cases, and defaults.
Architectural section markers ('--- Pure functions (Functional Core) ---' and '--- ContextMerger (Imperative Shell) ---') provide useful structural orientation.
ContextMerger class JSDoc documents the full 6-step pipeline, giving readers a high-level overview before diving into implementation.
ActivityType is a well-defined 7-value literal union -- good domain modeling for activity classification.
ContextSource.source uses a 5-value literal union instead of plain string -- prevents invalid source types at compile time.
Guard clauses and early returns are used effectively throughout (empty array checks, type validation fallback).
Named constants (DEFAULT_MAX_SOURCES, DEFAULT_IMPORTANCE, VALID_ACTIVITY_TYPES) used consistently instead of magic values.
All functions are short and focused: the longest (merge) is ~38 lines, well under the 50-line threshold. Cyclomatic complexity is low (1-4) across all functions.
Input validation at entry point: merge() immediately throws for empty sources array before any I/O, following fail-fast principle.
Tests include error-path coverage: both ContextMerger and EntityExtractor test LLM failure scenarios with proper error wrapping verification.
